ocr.text: The adoption of prefabrication and Building Information Modeling (BIM) technologies continues to shape the construction
landscape across Europe and beyond. As detailed in the accompanying data table, regions are experiencing varied levels of
engagement with these innovative practices. For instance, customer satisfaction rates in North America stand at an impressive
80%, indicating a strong acceptance of these methodologies among stakeholders. In contrast, Asia's customer satisfaction is
slightly lower at 78%, which underscores the potential for improvement in this market. The projected growth rate of 10% across
major regions reflects an optimistic forecast for the uptake of prefabrication techniques over the next few years. Notably, the
total cost savings for the industry are anticipated to reach 57%, presenting a compelling financial incentive for companies to

By 2024, Asia is projected to account for CHF75M in prefabrication expenditures, illustrating a significant commitment to
innovation. Furthermore, the total number of ongoing projects is currently marked as "InProgress," emphasizing the industry’s
momentum. As organizations strive to enhance efficiency and reduce construction costs, the overall percentage of projects

This data illustrates a transformative shift in how the construction sector approaches project delivery and client engagement.
